name: Flight to Michel Delving Rules
text: Scent attachments count as Condition attachments. Cards with Victory value go to the Victory Display after being explored, they don’t become scent attachments. When a character with any number of scent attachments leaves play, the attachments are discarded to the encounter discard pile.

name: Flight to Michel Delving 1
text: Double-unique character A double-unique character is a character that has a title containing two unique symbols, like “[unique] Fili & [unique] Kili”. It is treated as a single character during deckbuilding and gameplay, but it poses a uniqueness conflict with both the individual titles “Fili” and “Kili”. Cards effects that target to one of the titles, like “Attach to Kili” can also target a double-unique character contatining that title.

name: Thorin Oakenshield
text: Unique Dwarf allies you play do not require a resource match, and they collect 1 resource each resource phase. Resources in a unique Dwarf ally’s pool can only be spent to play attachments on that ally. While playing or resolving the effects of an attachment played in this way, that ally is considered to have the hero card type.

name: Dwalin
text: Response: When Dwalin would suffer damage, discard the top 2 cards of your deck. Either add 1 of those cards to your hand or cancel 1 point of that damage. (Limit once per phase.)

name: Into the Fray
text: Response: At the end of the planning phase, discard your hand and draw 3 cards.

name: Dwarven Fiddle
text: Attach to a Dwarf or Minstrel character. Restricted. Action: Exhaust an Instrument attachment to shuffle your deck, then look at and rearrange the top 3 cards. Exhaust a second Instrument attachment to choose a Dwarf or Minstrel ally from those cards and add it to your hand.

name: Book of Mazarbul
text: Attach to a hero or Ori. Response: After a second enemy engages you during a phase, exhaust Book of Mazarbul to draw 1 card. Response: When you are eliminated from the game, discard up to 3 non-unique enemies engaged with you and attach Book of Mazarbul to a hero controlled by another player.

name: Ori
text: Response: After you play Ori from your hand, search your deck for a Record attachment and add it to your hand. If you are engaged with an enemy, put it into play. Shuffle your deck.

name: Óin
text: Response: Exhaust a Weapon or Armor attached to Óin to cancel 1 damage just dealt to a character you control.

name: Wealth of Thrór
text: Attach to Thorin Oakenshield. Action: Discard 1 resource from a Dwarf character’s pool to give that character +1 [willpower], [attack], or [defense] until the end of the phase. (Limit twice per phase per character.)

name: Dwarven Vigour
text: Attach to a Dwarf character. Response: Spend 1 resource and shuffle Dwarven Vigour into your deck to cancel up to 2 points of damage just dealt to the attached character. Response: When Dwarven Vigour is discarded from your deck, attach it to a Dwarf character.

name: Mining Greedily
text: Setup: Each player that controls a Dwarf hero may raise their threat by 2 to opt in. If at least 3 players opt in, they each reduce their threat by 2.

name: Mining Greedily
text: Each location gains the Underground trait. If you opted in, event cards cannot shuffle cards into your deck, and if you have no cards in your deck, you are eliminated. Forced: At the beginning of the planning phase, each opted-in player discards the top 3 cards of their deck and may spend 1 resource to add one of those cards to their hand.

name: Being Tracked
text: Setup: Make Deephallow the active location. The first player adds On Our Heels and a copy of Ruffian Hunting Party to the staging area. Each other player searches the encounter deck for an enemy and adds it into play. Shuffle the 5 Shire locations and 8[pp] encounter cards together to form the encounter deck. Set the remaining encounter cards aside. Each player attaches the top card of the encounter deck facedown to a character they control as a scent attachment.

name: Being Tracked
text: Forced: After an enemy or a location leaves play, attach that card facedown as a Scent attachment to a character with the fewest Scent attachments. Forced: If the encounter deck and encounter discard pile are both empty, the players lose the game.

name: Sudden Downpour
text: When Revealed: Discard one Scent attachment from each hero in play. Search the encounter deck and discard pile for a copy of Muddy Road and put it into play. Place 1 progress on each copy of Muddy Road in play. If no location enters play this way, this card gains surge. Locations cannot be explored while in the staging area.

name: Lose the Pursuers
text: When Revealed: Each player attaches a scent card to a character they control.

name: Lose the Pursuers
text: Forced: After an enemy or a location leaves play, attach that card facedown as a Scent attachment to a character with the fewest Scent attachments. Forced: If the encounter deck and encounter discard pile are both empty, the players lose the game. Refresh Action: If this stage has at least 5[pp] progress, each player can discard any number of their characters. If those characters had more than half ot the total scent attachments in play, advance to stage 3A. Otherwise, advance to stage 4A.

name: Race to the Finish Line
text: When Revealed: Remove On Our Heels from play. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

name: Race to the Finish Line
text: Forced: At the beginning of the round, each player attaches the top card of the encounter deck to a character with the fewest scent attachments in play as a facedown Scent Attachment. Forced: If a scent card would be discarded, remove it from the game instead. Forced: If the encounter deck and encounter discard pile are both empty, the players lose the game. If the players defeat this stage, they have won the game.

name: Face the Pursuers
text: When Revealed: If On Our Heels is in play, flip it. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck.

name: Face the Pursuers
text: Forced: At the beginning of the round, each player attaches the top card of the encounter deck to a character with the fewest scent attachments in play as a facedown Scent Attachment. Forced: If a scent card would be discarded, remove it from the game instead. Forced: If the encounter deck and encounter discard pile are both empty, the players lose the game. If the players defeat this stage and Gaur the Burned is not in play, they have won the game.

name: Hunting Dogs
text: Forced: After a character defends an attack made by Hunting Dogs, attach the top card of the encounter deck to that character facedown as a scent attachment.
shadow: Shadow: Attacking enemy gets +1 [attack] (+2 [attack] instead if the defender has any Scent attachments).

name: Ruffian Hunting Party
text: X is equal to the number of characters controlled by the first player that have a scent card attached. Forced: After a character enters play, attach the top card of the encounter deck to it facedown as a scent attachment.

name: Unwashed Companions
text: When Revealed: Each character gets –1 [willpower] for each scent attachment it has until the end of the round. If there are no scent attachments in play, this card gains surge.
shadow: Shadow: Attacking enemy gets +X [attack], where X is the number of Scent attachments attached to the defending character.

name: Cunning Southerner
text: Forced: After a character attacks Cunning Southerner, attach the top card of the encounter deck to that character as a scent attachment.

name: Muddy Road
text: Muddy Road gets +2 [threat] for each progress on it. Forced: After a Weather treachery is revealed, add 1 progress to Muddy Road.

name: Cleansing Stream
text: Cleansing Stream gets +2 quest points for each resource token on it. Response: After Cleansing Stream becomes the active location, each player may choose to place 1 resource token on it to discard a Scent attachment from each hero they control.

name: Stinky Swamp
text: Travel: Each player attaches a scent card to a character they control with the fewest scent attachments.
shadow: Shadow: Attach the top card of the encounter deck to the defending character facedown as a Scent attachment.

name: Deephallow
text: While Deephallow is the active location, enemies do not make engagement checks. Forced: After Deephallow is explored, each player attaches a scent attachment to a character without one.

name: Soggy and Cold
text: When Revealed: Deal 1 damage to a hero and attach the top card of the encounter deck to it facedown as a scent attachment.
shadow: Shadow: Resolve the “when revealed” effect of this card.

name: The Weary Mud
text: When Revealed: Attach this card to the character with the highest-cost Item attachment in play facedown as a Scent attachment. You may discard that attachment to cancel this effect.

name: Footprints in the Mud
text: When Revealed: If there are no locations in the staging area, this card gains surge. Otherwise, add 1 progress to each location in the staging area with 2 or more remaining quest points. Each location in the staging area gets +X [threat] until the end of the round where X is the amount of tokens on it.

name: Stragglers Will Be Hunted
text: When Revealed: The first player either chooses and discards a character with the most scent attachments, or they deal 1 damage to each character they control with a scent attachment.
shadow: Shadow: If the defending character has any Scent attachments, the attacking enemy makes an additional attack.

name: On Our Heels
text: Action: Choose and reveal a Shire location that was removed from the game.

name: Gaur the Burned
text: Immune to player card effects. Cannot be optionally engaged. Only the character with the most scent attachments in play can attack Gaur the Burned. Forced: Whenever a scent card is attached to a character, Gaur the Burned engages its owner and makes an immediate attack. Trigger all shadow effects on scent cards attached to the defending character.

name: Willowbottom
text: Surge. When Revealed: You may remove Willowbottom from the game. If you don’t, shuffle up to 5 set aside encounter cards into the encounter deck. Response: After you travel to Willowbottom, each player may move a scent attachment attached to a character to a different character.
shadow: Shadow: Reveal this card, ignoring surge.

name: Thistle Brook
text: Surge. When Revealed: You may remove Thistle Brook from the game. If you don’t, shuffle up to 3 set aside encounter cards into the encounter deck. Response: After you travel to Thistle Brook, each player may remove a scent attachment attached to a character they control from the game.
shadow: Shadow: Reveal this card, ignoring surge.

name: Pincup
text: Surge. When Revealed: You may remove Pincup from the game. If you don’t, shuffle up to 6 set aside encounter cards into the encounter deck. Response: After you travel to Pincup, each player may remove 1 progress token from a location in play.
shadow: Shadow: Reveal this card, ignoring surge.

name: Tuckborough
text: Surge. When Revealed: You may remove Tuckborough from the game. If you don’t, shuffle up to 7 set aside encounter cards into the encounter deck. Response: After you travel to Tuckborough, each player may move a scent attachment attached to a character to an enemy engaged with them as a shadow card.
shadow: Shadow: Reveal this card, ignoring surge.

name: Tookbank
text: Surge. When Revealed: You may remove Tookbank from the game. If you don’t, shuffle up to 4 set aside encounter cards into the encounter deck. Response: After you travel to Tookbank, each player may return a character they control to their hand.
shadow: Shadow: Reveal this card, ignoring surge.
